data.body:
PASS 1 — T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, OVERLAY): PASS / not-applicable — no findings.

T1's bright-lines (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel architectural pattern, perf/scalability target, migration) do not fire for this plan. It is an incremental extension of the EXISTING, documented workflow editor and engine, not a novel architecture:

- Dependencies already vendored & in use — bpmn-js, bpmn-js-properties-panel, bpmn-moddle, the registered `rebar` moddle descriptor, and the Python IR↔BPMN round-trip (docs/workflow-editor.md). No new dependency is introduced; the plan says "confirm + extend only as needed."
- Prior-art consideration already on record in this area — `bpmn-auto-layout` was evaluated and rejected with a documented rationale (docs/workflow-editor.md "A readable layout").
- The constructs being made editable pre-exist in the engine: the `batch` step + prompt-library-backed `criteria` list with `when` (src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py `_run_batch`, executor.py `BatchRunner`/`DefaultBatchRunner`, lint.py, schema.py; sample in examples/review_skeleton.yaml) and the prompt step `if:`/`when` overlay predicate. The editor's config-carrying mechanism (`<rebar:Config>` extension + moddle registration) and properties-panel custom provider also pre-exist.
- No unverified capability assertions: the load-bearing claims (the e6d9 editor invariant — clean git diffs + schema-validated round-trip; moddle-faithful round-trip) map to existing, documented code/tests.

Per the criterion's ANTI-FP guidance, a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search and T1 is not-applicable when no bright-line fires. Affirmation: criteria T1 satisfied (not-applicable, clean).
